L1.area: JDBC connection management and data source resolution utilities

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where a JNDI lookup is performed with an attacker-controlled URL without validating the URL scheme. This allows an attacker to specify LDAP or RMI URLs that cause the application to load and deserialize arbitrary objects from a remote server, leading to remote code execution.

L3.area: JDBC utility layer handling driver loading and JNDI-based data source resolution
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where a JNDI lookup is performed with an attacker-controlled URL without validating the URL scheme. When the H2 database console or linked table feature accepts a connection string specifying a JNDI context driver, an attacker can provide LDAP or RMI URLs that cause the application to load and deserialize arbitrary objects from a remote server, leading to remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The JdbcUtils.getConnection method performs a JNDI lookup using an attacker-controlled URL without validating the URL scheme, allowing LDAP/RMI URLs to trigger remote class loading and deserialization of untrusted objects, leading to RCE.
